title: Accessibility
lede: WCAG 2.1 contrast for every brand color, approved pairings, type minimums for digital and print, and a reviewer checklist.

matrix.title: 16.1 — Contrast ratio matrix
matrix.desc: Every brand color paired against every background. Numbers are WCAG 2.1 contrast ratios; pills mark the highest passing grade.

pairs.title: 16.2 — Approved pairings
pairs.desc: Six recommended foreground / background pairs with usage notes. Three more pairs (KIMES red on white for body, Beauty&Derma pink on white, INSPIRE lime on white) are flagged and must not be used for body copy.
pairs.sampleBody: The KIMES brand at 13px body — does this pairing hold up at the smallest size we ship?

items.name: Ink on White
items.note: Default body and headline pairing. Highest contrast.

items.name: White on Ink
items.note: Inverse — used for dark hero, sponsor reels.

items.name: White on KIMES Red
items.note: Primary CTA. Passes AA for ≥18pt; borderline for body.

items.name: KIMES Red on White
items.note: Headlines only — fails AA for body. Use ≥24pt or bold ≥18pt.

items.name: White on MC Blue
items.note: Medicomtek primary. Strong AA on all sizes.

items.name: Ink on INSPIRE Lime
items.note: Inspire combo — passes AAA, the highest-contrast brand pair.

types.title: 16.3 — Type minimums
types.desc: Minimum sizes for digital, signage, and print surfaces. Always pair size with appropriate weight — light weights need more size to remain legible.

items.use: Body text on web. 16px minimum on digital surfaces; 14px allowed for dense tables.
Captions, metadata, timestamps. Below 12px is reserved for monospace tokens only.
Buttons and tab labels. Always paired with min 44×44 hit target.
Stage screens, large-format signage. Anything below 24pt is illegible from row 5.
Hero, on-stage headlines. Always Montserrat 700 / Pretendard 800.
Print collateral floor. Below 9 pt requires regulatory approval (legal, citations).

checklist.title: 16.4 — Reviewer checklist
checklist.desc: Eight items to validate before shipping any brand surface. Mix of do's (green) and don'ts (red).

items.title: Use Ink on White by default
items.body: The 13.6:1 pairing is your most reliable surface. Everything else needs justification.

items.title: Don't set body in KIMES red
items.body: Red on white is 4.0:1 — fails AA for normal body. Use red only for headlines ≥18pt or bold ≥14pt.

items.title: Add a focus ring to every interactive element
items.body: 2px solid #E60012, 2px offset. Visible in keyboard tab order; never rely on hover state alone.

items.title: Don't use color alone to convey state
items.body: Pair red error states with an icon and text label. Pair green success states the same way.

items.title: Provide alt text on every wordmark instance
items.body: Decorative SVGs should be aria-hidden. Brand wordmarks must announce the brand name.

items.title: Don't auto-play motion above 2 cycles
items.body: Provide a pause control on any loop. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.

items.title: Maintain 44×44 minimum hit targets
items.body: On mobile, any tappable element must be ≥44×44. Match WCAG 2.5.5.

items.title: Don't place text over busy imagery without a scrim
items.body: Add a 60% black scrim or 92% white overlay so the text-to-bg contrast holds.
